title: lichen_util.h:27-28 tc_sha256_init return not checked separately
description:
In lichen_sha256() at lines 27-31:
```c
if (tc_sha256_init(&state) != TC_CRYPTO_SUCCESS ||
    tc_sha256_update(&state, input, inlen) != TC_CRYPTO_SUCCESS ||
    tc_sha256_final(output, &state) != TC_CRYPTO_SUCCESS) {
    ret = -EIO;
}
```

The short-circuit evaluation means if init() fails, update() and final() are never called. This is correct behavior - no point continuing after init failure.

The TinyCrypt SHA-256 functions only fail on NULL arguments:
- tc_sha256_init: returns 0 if state is NULL
- tc_sha256_update: returns 0 if state or data is NULL  
- tc_sha256_final: returns 0 if digest or state is NULL

All pointers are checked before this call (lines 21-23), so these can't fail in practice. The -EIO return is for defensive completeness.

NO BUG - defensive error handling is correct.
